rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical chlorotrifluoromethylation (and bromotrifluoromethylation) of alkenes via anodically coupled electrolysis combining two parallel oxidative events
electrochemical_role: Anode performs two parallel oxidations: direct oxidation of CF3SO2- (Langlois reagent) to generate transient CF3 radical, and oxidation of Mn(II)-Cl to a persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl radical chlorinating species; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: benzylic/alkyl C-centered radical (I) after CF3 addition to alkene

electron_flow: At the anode, CF3SO2- is oxidized (Ep/2 = 800 mV) losing SO2 to give CF3 radical, while in parallel Mn(II)-Cl is oxidized (E = 780 mV) to persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl; CF3 radical (transient) rapidly adds to the alkene to give a C-centered radical (I), which is then trapped by the persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl via atom transfer to deliver the chloro-trifluoromethylated product and regenerate Mn(II). Protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Likely the anodic oxidation events (cell potential rises to 1.3-1.6 V to oxidize both CF3SO2- and Mn(II)-Cl); product-forming radical addition (path A) is fast and not rate-limiting

probe_reagents.name: vinyl cyclopropane (25)
probe_reagents.observation: Ring-opened product 26 obtained (40%, E/Z 10:1), consistent with C-radical intermediate after CF3 addition and before chlorination

probe_reagents.name: bisallyl tosylamine (27)
probe_reagents.observation: 5-exo-trig cyclized pyrrolidine 28 (82%, dr 3.5:1), confirming radical intermediate, excluding cationic ECEC pathway

probe_reagents.name: MgCl2 vs LiCl swap
probe_reagents.observation: LiCl gave diminished 2a yield with substantial dichloride 2d (12%), indicating chemoselectivity depends on solubility/Mn coordination

probe_reagents.name: Mn omission control
probe_reagents.observation: No 2a formed without Mn(OAc)2, demonstrating essential role of Mn-Cl persistent radical

probe_reagents.name: cyclic voltammetry of [Mn(III)]-Cl/[Mn(II)]-Cl
probe_reagents.observation: Reversible redox waves indicate lifetime of [Mn(III)]-Cl >10^2 s (persistent radical character)

notes: Mechanism leverages persistent-radical effect: transient CF3 radical (spin on C) adds to alkene faster than the persistent [Mn(III)]-Cl (path A > path C), and cross-coupling of C-radical with persistent Mn-Cl (path D) outcompetes radical-radical homocoupling (path B), giving selective hetero-difunctionalization. Alternative ECEC (carbocation) mechanism ruled out by radical clock results since required carbocations would be primary/destabilized. Side product 2d (vicinal dichloride) arises from competing Mn-mediated dichlorination pathway; dimerization of I suppressed by high local concentration of [Mn(III)]-Cl in the anodic diffusion layer.
